examples: Each subatomic particle has its antiparticle, its properties being equal and opposite to those of the particle.
When a particle encounters its antiparticle, they "annihilate" each other, disappearing in a high-energy flash of light.
To release it, just allow an antiparticle to come into contact with normal matter, and then capture the energy released.
When it does so, each particle of matter produced is accompanied by an appropriate antiparticle.
B0 pair, it will also allow the operators to work out which is the particle and which the antiparticle.
